programmes.show_description: ...Penelope Keith: Brand new series - In Chillingham, Britain's most haunted castle reveals its secrets. The owners of Iford Manor buy a pub. And Whitmore Hall has a spring clean. (Ep8)
Patients on the nightshift include a stabbing victim, an 86-year-old woman who's spent her life alone, and a former lawyer who's lost his wife, family and home to alcoholism.
The medical team, patients and their families face the meaning of life and death. One patient, 77-year-old John, faces a dangerous operation for a potentially deadly aneurysm.
...Snacks Aisle: How many mangoes are in a dried mango pack? The team also crack the secret of caramel popcorn and the mystery of snowballs' fluffy centre. (Ep4/4)

The campers pitch up at at the foot of Scotland's most famous mountain at Ben Nevis Holiday Park, where they take aim at a five-star rating with axe throwing and mountain biking.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien upcycle some stained glass and a Victorian hay rack, to try to turn a profit for their owners.
Leah Charles-King is in Mijas Costa helping Susan and Terry to find their forever home. They've given Leah a budget of £120,000 to find their perfect two-bedroom property near the beach.
County Durham. Hairdresser Julie plans a no-frills menu that she can prepare in advance. There's a tangle at the table when David makes a nasty comment about Stephen's dreadlocks. (Ep1/5)
County Durham. Beauty school teacher Ashleigh serves up sophisticated grub with a side order of hearts and love. (Ep2/5)
County Durham. Part-time farmer Stephen invites his guests to an evening on his farm, complete with a drinks reception in the stables and rustic home-cooked cuisine. (Ep3/5)
County Durham. Prison transport officer Tina's sophisticated French menu includes salmon crêpes and posh peas, with expectations high after her criticisms earlier in the week. (Ep4/5)
County Durham. IT manager David hosts a French evening, but can he keep Ashleigh and Tina away from each other's throats? (Ep5/5)
The first hosts, Ronnie and Steph, welcome their rivals to the Inn at the Park in Aberdeen, where one guest's culinary background raises the stakes at breakfast.
Shauna and Fiona host at Ingliston Country Club in Renfrewshire, where dated décor and a delayed breakfast lead to feedback that causes a storm in a teacup.
Steve and Allard host at the B&B they built themselves, 1692 Wasdale, in the Lake District. Shauna and Fiona think it's picture-perfect, but John has concerns about cleanliness.
The final visit is to the Lynbar Hotel, where confident John boasts that he serves the best breakfast in Blackpool. But do his guests agree?
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time to find out how much they've been paid and to settle some scores, before the winner is announced.
Mazda MX5: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue a 25-year-old example of the world's bestselling two-seater sports car: the Mazda MX5. But it's been off the road for some time.
Range Rover Vogue SE: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Cambridge to rescue a cherished but tired and rusty 1991 Range Rover Vogue SE that needs some TLC.
The team at picturesque doggy sanctuary Animal Welfare League NSW hope that neo-mastiff cross Goober's drool won't be too much for Scott and Adelaide.
Furniture maker Lucinda wants to construct a beautifully crafted, two-storey, highly-insulated timber home in woodland in South Herefordshire... without using heavy machinery.
Duncan and Liz build a revolutionary Passivhaus Premium powerhouse of a home in the Cotswolds. But with a spiralling schedule and lonesome living, will it all be worth it in the end?
There's a variety of sport and drink-related injuries on the Saturday shift. And when a family party goes horribly wrong, a dad and son sustain life-threatening injuries.
The paramedics help a child who's having an asthma attack, a young woman who has multiple sclerosis and is in severe pain, and a man with bowel cancer who's struggling to breathe.
The paramedics help an intoxicated woman found by the side of a road, an amateur boxer with a possible concussion, a woman in premature labour, and a woman who is unresponsive.
There's a variety of sport and drink-related injuries on the Saturday shift. And when a family party goes horribly wrong, a dad and son sustain life-threatening injuries.
William has broken his leg in three places. Nick's fallen from a 10-metre ladder onto concrete. And Wilfred, who lives alone, has had a nasty accident while cutting vegetables.
What's the secret to the bubbles in bubble tea? How come sour milk's bad but soured cream is great? And, from Portugal, the benefits of canned fish.

At Dalraddy Holiday Park on a traditional Scottish estate at Aviemore, the campers take a train ride, enjoy some quirky Highland games and battle it out on the paintball field.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn unwanted possessions into cash for their owners. In this episode, an old bureau gets a makeover in Yorkshire.
Gill and Paul dream of owning a holiday home in Benalmádena on Spain's Costa del Sol, to escape to during the winter months. Laura Hamilton shows them what their £110k can buy.
Basingstoke. The first host, marketing manager and maths enthusiast Nicole, takes her guests on a culinary journey from Lebanon to the Caribbean, with a disco for good measure. (Ep1/5)
Basingstoke. Cheeky business owner Adam brings fun to his fiesta with authentic Mexican cuisine, inspired by his one-week holiday to Cancún, and a water pistol gun fight. (Ep2/5)
Basingstoke. DJ Mark hosts a festival-themed evening and offers his guests a taste of his own special filtered water. Will the good vibes flow or will Mark's night be a washout? (Ep3/5)
Basingstoke. Winery worker Izzy hosts a night of sophistication, but Mark rejects the black-tie dress code. Can she give her guests a night to remember, or is it too much for some? (Ep4/5)
Basingstoke. Chris wraps up the week with a 90s music theme and a 'Franglais' menu. But with simmering tensions between Mark and Izzy, can a lip sync battle win over his guests? (Ep5/5)
Environmentally-friendly couple Ian and Jayne host first, at The School House B&B in Pencader, Wales. At dinner, eyebrows are raised at two straight-talking guests.
Neil and Jo welcome their rivals to Cathedral 73 in Cardiff. Their guests are wowed by the glamorous rooms, but the extra charges for parking and breakfast prove hard to swallow.
Golfer Raj hosts at the Leafy Suburban B&B in Hertfordshire, where the house rules include removing your shoes at the door. Neil and Jo aren't impressed by a surprise visitor in their bed.
The final visit is to The Wellington Inn in Hertfordshire, where resources manager Felicity Rainbow's spiritual approach to hosting includes cleansing the rooms' auras.
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time to find out how much they've been paid and to settle some scores. After bitter rebukes, the winner is announced.
A French bulldog called Kevin and a nervous terrier compete to win the hearts of a loved-up couple. And Rocco the Jack Russell meets an adventurous family who might just fit.
Australian shepherd Alfie and the Holmes family are on track to make a new bond, while Missy's attachment issues could be too much for a first-time owner.
Clare Balding presents live from Birmingham as the first Agility final raises the heart rate in the Main Arena. And the highly skilful Heelwork to Music competition gets underway.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back on some clever builds. In Surrey, Pippa's sick of her 'tatty little bungalow'. Can Kirstie help her fall back in love with her home?
Retired racehorse breeder John builds a million-pound, high-tech, accessible glass pavilion with a wildlife garden for him and his wife Helen, whose mobility was badly affected by a stroke.
Brand new series: Miles has spent thousands on fake designer furniture, but is one piece worth millions? A snuff box catches a dealer's eye, but can owner Gill bear to part with it? (Ep4/6)
Doctors Firas and André operate on a 78-year-old who has a life-threatening gut condition. Other patients include a man who twisted his knee moon-walking in a club.
Omar, who's 88, is brought to King's College Hospital after experiencing severe stomach pains. And staff battle to save an 18-year-old who's been stabbed in a street fight.
